data.description:

The Berkeley Group Holdings ( (GB:BKG) ) has shared an update.

The Berkeley Group Holdings plc has repurchased 45,000 of its ordinary shares for cancellation on 21 April 2026, executing the buyback via Barclays Bank within the price range of 3,432p to 3,482p per share. This transaction forms part of the authority granted by shareholders and reflects the company’s ongoing capital return strategy.

Following the cancellation of these shares, Berkeley’s total shares in issue stand at 93,543,063, a new denominator for regulatory disclosure of significant shareholdings. The reduced share count marginally enhances earnings per share and may support the stock’s valuation, while requiring investors to reassess any notification thresholds under U.K. transparency rules.

The most recent analyst rating on (GB:BKG) stock is a Sell with a £2990.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on The Berkeley Group Holdings stock, see the GB:BKG Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, BKG is a Outperform.

The overall stock score is primarily driven by strong valuation metrics, including a low P/E ratio and high dividend yield, which suggest potential undervaluation. However, technical indicators reflect a bearish trend, and financial performance highlights challenges in revenue growth and cash flow generation. Recent positive corporate events, while not weighted, support a positive outlook.

To see Spark’s full report on BKG stock, click here.

More about The Berkeley Group Holdings

The Berkeley Group Holdings plc is a U.K.-listed residential property developer focused on building homes and mixed-use developments, primarily in London and the South East of England. It is a constituent of the FTSE indices and is known for returning excess capital to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks.

data.title: Lottomatica Approves 2025 Accounts and Sets €0.44 Dividend
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lottomatica Group S.P.A. ( (IT:LTMC) ) has issued an announcement.

Lottomatica Group S.p.A. shareholders approved the 2025 annual financial statements and authorized a dividend distribution of €0.44 per outstanding ordinary share, excluding any treasury shares held at the record date. The dividend will be paid on 20 May 2026 via intermediaries in the Monte Titoli system, with the ex-dividend date set for 18 May and the record date for 19 May.

The company also announced that the minutes of the shareholders’ meeting and the updated Articles of Association will be made publicly available at its registered office and on its website, as well as through the authorized storage mechanism 1Info. These steps formalize the corporate decisions taken at the meeting and provide shareholders and the market with updated governance documentation.

The most recent analyst rating on (IT:LTMC) stock is a Buy with a EUR31.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Lottomatica Group S.P.A. stock, see the IT:LTMC Stock Forecast page.

More about Lottomatica Group S.P.A.

Lottomatica Group S.p.A., based in Rome, operates in the gaming and lottery sector, providing betting and related gaming services to the Italian market. The company focuses on regulated gaming activities and serves a wide base of retail and online players across Italy.

Average Trading Volume: 1,091,280

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: €6.79B

NCC Group has disclosed that several senior executives, including CEO Mike Maddison, CFO Guy Ellis, CMO Angela Brown and Escode managing director Jayesh Patel, have made small monthly purchases of ordinary shares under the company’s UK Share Incentive Plan. The transactions, executed on 17 April 2026 on the London Stock Exchange at a price of £1.2047 per share, signal ongoing alignment of management’s interests with shareholders through regular equity participation.

While the volumes involved are modest, the consistent participation of top leadership in the share plan underscores a governance approach that ties executive rewards to the company’s market performance. data.title: Egetis Therapeutics to Raise SEK 350 Million in Directed Share Issue to Fund Emcitate US Launch
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Egetis Therapeutics AB ( (SE:EGTX) ).

Egetis Therapeutics plans to raise approximately SEK 350 million through a directed share issue to international and Swedish institutional investors, led by Stifel as Sole Global Coordinator and supported by Van Lanschot Kempen and DNB Carnegie as Joint Bookrunners. The board prefers this accelerated, non-pre-emptive placement over a rights issue, citing faster execution, lower cost, reduced market risk, and the strategic benefit of strengthening the institutional investor base.

Proceeds are earmarked primarily for U.S. commercialization preparations for Emcitate, including building commercial and medical affairs infrastructure, advancing indication expansion into Resistance to Thyroid Hormone type beta, and funding working capital and general corporate purposes. The company expects the funds to carry it through to key Emcitate milestones, including a U.S. FDA decision in late September 2026, potential Priority Review Voucher issuance and monetization, and a planned U.S. launch in the fourth quarter of 2026, while adopting 90-day lock-up commitments for the company, board and senior management to support market confidence.

More about Egetis Therapeutics AB

Egetis Therapeutics AB is a Sweden-based biopharmaceutical company listed on Nasdaq Stockholm that focuses on developing and commercializing treatments for rare endocrine and metabolic diseases. Its lead product candidate, Emcitate (tiratricol), targets severe thyroid-related disorders, with an initial focus on the U.S. market and potential indication expansion into Resistance to Thyroid Hormone type beta.

Average Trading Volume: 804,759

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: SEK2.28B

For an in-depth examination of EGTX stock, go to TipRanks’ Overview page.

An announcement from Baillie Gifford European Growth Trust PLC GBP ( (GB:BGEU) ) is now available.

Baillie Gifford European Growth Trust plc has repurchased 200,000 ordinary shares at 100.08 pence each, with the shares to be held in treasury rather than cancelled. This buyback increases the company’s treasury holdings to 108,438,323 shares and leaves 294,005,367 shares in issue, a figure investors must now use when calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA transparency rules.

The transaction underscores the trust’s continued use of share buybacks as a capital management tool, which can help manage the discount to net asset value and potentially enhance value for remaining shareholders. It also clarifies the updated share capital structure, giving stakeholders an accurate basis for reporting significant holdings and any changes in their interests in the trust.

data.title: M&G Credit Income Trust Stays Defensive as Geopolitics Pressure Credit Markets
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

M&G Credit Income Investment Trust Plc ( (GB:MGCI) ) just unveiled an announcement.

M&G Credit Income Investment Trust reported a net asset value total return of 0.41% for the first quarter of 2026, significantly trailing its benchmark as its deliberately defensive stance and wider credit spreads weighed on performance amid heightened geopolitical and macroeconomic stress. The managers emphasised that the portfolio remains largely investment grade with minimal software and US exposure, and they continue to avoid chasing yield in what they see as an expensive credit market.

The trust raised capital through the issuance of 5.8 million new shares early in the year and deployed proceeds into a mix of public bonds and private deals, including a data centre, regulatory capital transaction, logistics assets in the Benelux region and a UK packaging business. After the share price moved to a discount in March, the company restarted buybacks under its Zero Discount Policy, trimmed exposure to a secured asset-backed credit fund, and kept a sizeable allocation to high-quality ABS funds and a £40 million credit facility to capitalise on potential dislocations as energy-driven stagflation risks and geopolitical uncertainty threaten to unsettle credit markets further.

Spark’s Take on MGCI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MGCI is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by strong balance sheet quality and an attractive dividend yield, offset by inconsistent cash flow generation and weak technical momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD).

To see Spark’s full report on MGCI stock, click here.

More about M&G Credit Income Investment Trust Plc

M&G Credit Income Investment Trust plc is a London-listed investment trust focused on generating income from a diversified portfolio of public and private credit, including investment grade bonds, asset-backed securities and private loans. The trust primarily targets opportunities in the UK and Europe, aiming to deliver returns over SONIA through active credit selection and disciplined risk management.

Average Trading Volume: 511,225

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Robert Half ( (RHI) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 20, 2026, Robert Half amended and restated severance agreements with its named executive officers, including M. Keith Waddell, Michael C. Buckley, Paul F. Gentzkow, Joseph A. Tarantino and Harold M. Messmer. The revisions removed provisions granting severance benefits upon a voluntary termination of employment following a change in control, leaving other material terms intact to better align executive pay practices with prevailing market norms and governance expectations.

The company’s decision to narrow severance eligibility for top leaders reflects a shift toward more shareholder-friendly compensation structures. By curbing payouts triggered by voluntary departures after a change in control, Robert Half reduces potential costs tied to future transactions and signals responsiveness to evolving best practices in executive contracts, which could influence investor perceptions of its governance standards.

The most recent analyst rating on (RHI) stock is a Sell with a $20.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Robert Half stock, see the RHI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RHI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RHI is a Neutral.

The score is primarily constrained by weakening financial performance (multi-year revenue declines and major margin compression) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with weak momentum). These are partly offset by a strong financial foundation and cash generation, plus an attractive dividend yield, while the latest call suggests sequential improvement but continued near-term profitability and EPS pressure.

To see Spark’s full report on RHI stock, click here.

data.title: Cloetta AGM Approves Dividend, Higher Board Fees and New Share-Based Incentive Plan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cloetta AB ( ($SE:CLA.B) ) has shared an announcement.

Cloetta’s annual general meeting in Stockholm approved the 2025 accounts and confirmed a dividend of SEK 1.40 per share, with a total payout of about SEK 401 million to shareholders. The meeting also discharged the board and CEO from liability and re-elected all seven directors, including chairman Morten Falkenberg, while significantly increasing board fees and maintaining existing committee remuneration levels.

Shareholders reappointed PwC as auditor and endorsed a new long-term share-based incentive plan for up to 74 senior employees, requiring personal share investments and granting tiered performance share rights tied to long-term value targets. As an alternative to using treasury shares, the board plans a share swap with a bank to deliver shares under the LTI 2026 scheme, reinforcing Cloetta’s focus on management alignment with shareholder interests and long-term performance.

More about Cloetta AB

Cloetta AB is a Swedish confectionery company that produces and markets chocolates, candies and other sugar confectionery products. The group focuses on branded consumer sweets across the Nordic region and selected European markets, distributing through retail channels and leveraging well-known local brands.

Average Trading Volume: 455,323

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: SEK15.07B
